page: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 480 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 21428.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 82984.85, both placing in the top tier. Single-core performance is also notable, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0. In memory tests, the DDR5 subsystem recorded a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 5134.19 GB/sec and a large block uncached read bandwidth of 196.82 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are highlighted by a PassMark CPU encryption score of 72589.74 and a ray tracing score of 91339.0.

The combination of high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe SSD storage, and advanced instruction sets makes the c8id.16xlarge highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads. It excels in software compilation, as evidenced by a top-tier Geekbench clang compilation score of 59025.0, and static web serving, which reached a throughput of 109.49 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities for medium-scale language model inference, processing 7B parameter model prompts at 296.09 tokens per second. However, with a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB, it is less suited for memory-bound databases or massive datasets that exceed the 128.0 GB RAM capacity, representing a clear tradeoff in favor of raw compute density and local storage speed.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and static web serving, alongside strong memory bandwidth. With a baseline network speed of 30 Gbps and high-speed local storage, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, including software development, web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference, though its 2.0 GB per core memory ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier performance for compilation and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Includes 3800 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and compute-heavy workloads
